title: crataegus × oxyacanthoides subsp. calciphila
intro: Crataegus × oxyacanthoides subsp. calciphila, also known as a hybrid hawthorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and cardiovascular protective effects. The health benefits associated with this plant include reducing blood pressure, improving heart function, and preventing the formation of blood clots, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular diseases. The herbal preparations of Crataegus × oxyacanthoides subsp. calciphila can be found in the form of capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart-related conditions.
